problem_text_cn: 古希腊哲学家芝诺提出的神话英雄阿齐尔斯永远追不上乌龟的著名论证称为芝诺佯谬。论证中芝诺采用了两种不同的时间度量。任何一种具有重复性的过程都可以作为“钟”，用其重复的次数来量度时间，芝诺除了采用“普通”的钟度量时间 $t$ 以外，还采用了一种奇特的钟，该钟使用的重复性过程是：阿齐尔斯逐次到达乌龟在前一次的出发点。我们暂时称这种钟为芝诺钟，它测得的时间记为 $t'$。设阿齐尔斯和乌龟在开始时相距为 $L$，阿齐尔斯的速度为 $v_1$，乌龟的速度为 $v_2$，且 $v_1 > v_2$。如果用普通的钟，阿齐尔斯将在 $t = L/(v_1 - v_2)$ 时，赶上乌龟。当 $t > L/(v_1 - v_2)$ 时，阿齐尔斯就超过乌龟了。我们的任务是要求出普通时 $t$ 和芝诺时 $t'$ 之间的变换关系式。
problem_text_en: The famous argument proposed by the ancient Greek philosopher Zeno, in which the mythical hero Achilles can never catch up with a tortoise, is known as Zeno's paradox. In the argument, Zeno used two different time measurements. Any repetitive process can be used as a "clock", measuring time by the number of repetitions. Besides using the "ordinary" clock to measure time $t$, Zeno also used a peculiar clock whose repetitive process is: Achilles successively reaches the starting point of the tortoise from the previous step. We temporarily call this clock the Zeno clock, and the time it measures is denoted as $t'$. Suppose Achilles and the tortoise are initially separated by a distance $L$, Achilles' speed is $v_1$, and the tortoise's speed is $v_2$, with $v_1 > v_2$. Using the ordinary clock, Achilles will catch up with the tortoise at $t = L/(v_1 - v_2)$. When $t > L/(v_1 - v_2)$, Achilles overtakes the tortoise. Our task is to find the transformation relationship between the ordinary time $t$ and the Zeno time $t'$.
problem_solution_cn:
【分析与解】我们采用列表的方法找出 $t$ 和 $t'$ 的关系，在表2-1中，一边是芝诺时 $t'$，另一边是普通时 $t$。

当 $t'=1$ 时，阿齐尔斯到达乌龟在 $t'=0$ 时出发点；当 $t'=2$ 时，阿齐尔斯到达乌龟在 $t'=1$ 时的出发点。一般地，当 $t'=n$ 时，阿齐尔斯到达 $t'=n-1$ 时的出发点。显然，只有当 $t'\to\infty$ 时，阿齐尔斯才能无限接近乌龟，对于任何有限的 $t'$，阿齐尔斯总是落在乌龟的后面，这就是芝诺的结论。当然，$t'\to\infty$ 是芝诺时的无限。

在我们采用的普通时 $t$，它与 $t'$ 的对应关系，如表2-1。

从表中得到 $t'=n$ 时，对应的
$$
t = \frac{L}{v_1}\left[1 + \frac{v_2}{v_1} + \dots + \left(\frac{v_2}{v_1}\right)^{n-1}\right] = \frac{L}{v_1} \frac{1 - \left(\frac{v_2}{v_1}\right)^n}{1 - \frac{v_2}{v_1}} = \frac{L}{v_1 - v_2}\left[1 - \left(\frac{v_2}{v_1}\right)^n\right]
$$
所以 $t$ 和 $t'$ 的变换关系式为
$$
t = \frac{L}{v_1 - v_2}\left[1 - \left(\frac{v_2}{v_1}\right)^{t'}\right]
$$
此式称为芝诺变换。当 $t'\to\infty$ 时
$$
t = \frac{L}{v_1 - v_2}
$$
problem_solution_en:
【Analysis and Solution】We use a tabular method to find the relationship between $t$ and $t'$. In Table 2-1, one side is the Zeno time $t'$ and the other is the ordinary time $t$.

When $t'=1$, Achilles reaches the tortoise's starting point at $t'=0$; when $t'=2$, Achilles reaches the tortoise's starting point at $t'=1$. In general, when $t'=n$, Achilles reaches the starting point at $t'=n-1$. Obviously, only when $t'\to\infty$ can Achilles infinitely approach the tortoise; for any finite $t'$, Achilles always lags behind the tortoise, which is Zeno's conclusion. Of course, $t'\to\infty$ is the infinity in Zeno time.

In the ordinary time $t$ we use, its correspondence with $t'$ is shown in Table 2-1.

From the table, when $t'=n$, the corresponding $t$ is:
$$
t = \frac{L}{v_1}\left[1 + \frac{v_2}{v_1} + \dots + \left(\frac{v_2}{v_1}\right)^{n-1}\right] = \frac{L}{v_1} \frac{1 - \left(\frac{v_2}{v_1}\right)^n}{1 - \frac{v_2}{v_1}} = \frac{L}{v_1 - v_2}\left[1 - \left(\frac{v_2}{v_1}\right)^n\right]
$$
Thus, the transformation relationship between $t$ and $t'$ is:
$$
t = \frac{L}{v_1 - v_2}\left[1 - \left(\frac{v_2}{v_1}\right)^{t'}\right]
$$
This equation is called the Zeno transformation. When $t'\to\infty$:
$$
t = \frac{L}{v_1 - v_2}
$$
img_cn:
芝诺时 $t'$ 与普通时 $t$ 的对应关系表（表2-1）

作直线运动的质点位置随时间变化的关系，可以在 $x-t$ 坐标中用一条曲线（称位置图线）来表示（图2-3）。平均速度即为割线 $\overline{AB}$ 的斜率。当 $\Delta t \to 0$ 时，$B$ 点无限靠近 $A$ 点，割线变成了过 $A$ 点的切线。因此，速度等于过曲线上一点切线的斜率，它是位置对时间的瞬时变化率。

作直线运动的质点的速度随时间的变化关系，可以在 $v-t$ 坐标中用一条曲线（称速度图线）来表示（图2-4）。平均加速度和瞬时加速度分别是过曲线两点割线的斜率和曲线上一点切线的斜率。

质点沿三维空间曲线由 $\boldsymbol{r}(t_1)=\boldsymbol{r}(t)$ 运动到 $\boldsymbol{r}(t_2)=\boldsymbol{r}(t+\Delta t)$（图2-5），其平均速度为 $\boldsymbol{v} = \dfrac{\boldsymbol{r}(t+\Delta t)-\boldsymbol{r}(t)}{\Delta t} = \dfrac{\Delta \boldsymbol{r}}{\Delta t}$。
质点作三维空间曲线运动时，其瞬时速度方向必为曲线的切线方向。速度增量 $\Delta \boldsymbol{v}$ 如图2-6(a)、(b)所示。
img_en:
Table 2-1: The correspondence between Zeno time $t'$ and ordinary time $t$

The position of a particle moving in a straight line as a function of time can be represented by a curve (called a position graph) in the $x-t$ coordinate system (Figure 2-3). The average velocity is the slope of the secant $\overline{AB}$. As $\Delta t \to 0$, point $B$ approaches point $A$ infinitely, and the secant becomes the tangent at point $A$. Thus, velocity equals the slope of the tangent at a point on the curve, which is the instantaneous rate of change of position with respect to time.

The velocity of a particle moving in a straight line as a function of time can be represented by a curve (called a velocity graph) in the $v-t$ coordinate system (Figure 2-4). The average acceleration and instantaneous acceleration are the slopes of the secant through two points on the curve and the tangent at a point on the curve, respectively.

A particle moves along a three-dimensional space curve from $\boldsymbol{r}(t_1)=\boldsymbol{r}(t)$ to $\boldsymbol{r}(t_2)=\boldsymbol{r}(t+\Delta t)$ (Figure 2-5), its average velocity is $\boldsymbol{v} = \dfrac{\boldsymbol{r}(t+\Delta t)-\boldsymbol{r}(t)}{\Delta t} = \dfrac{\Delta \boldsymbol{r}}{\Delta t}$.

When a particle moves along a three-dimensional space curve, the direction of its instantaneous velocity must be the tangent direction of the curve. The velocity increment $\Delta \boldsymbol{v}$ is shown in Figure 2-6(a), (b).
problem_answer: 普通时 $t$ 和芝诺时 $t'$ 之间的变换关系式为 $t = \dfrac{L}{v_1 - v_2}\left[1 - \left(\dfrac{v_2}{v_1}\right)^{t'}\right]$。
